\rhead{\small RGCS Crystal Application}

\title{\vspace{-2.0em}\textbf{The RGCS Crystal Application, Version~3:\\
Anisotropic Propagation, the Node Menu,\\ and the Optical Probe Layer}}
\author{Andrew Green}
\date{2026}

\begin{document}
\maketitle

\begin{abstract}
Version~3 preserves every validated v2 result (geometry, resonance
ladder, coupled modes, loading, coherence, uncertainty --- all frozen)
and adds three tested layers: anisotropic elastic propagation via the
Christoffel eigenproblem \eqid{RSCS-O.17}, which resolves the v2 scalar
wave-speed hypothesis into a measured-orientation model; an explicit
eight-entry \emph{node menu} that treats ``the eye'' as a modeling choice
to be measured, not a fact; and an optical probe layer (quartz indices,
photoelastic response, ray/path model) with pre-registered claims whose
directional entries are expected \emph{nulls}. Experimental status is
stated plainly: the physical hypotheses of the programme remain
unconfirmed; what this application provides is a reproducible model stack
and a falsification plan.
\end{abstract}

\section{Forward hierarchy}
\begin{figure}[H]
\centering
\begin{tikzpicture}[
  box/.style={draw, rounded corners=2pt, align=center, font=\small,
              inner sep=4.5pt, fill=soft},
  arr/.style={-{Stealth[length=2.4mm]}, thick},
  node distance=5.5mm]
\node[box] (geo) {Measured geometry + orientation \EST};
\node[box, below=of geo] (mat) {Material model: $C_{ij}$, $\rho$
  \EST{} (handbook, D5-002)};
\node[box, below=of mat] (prop) {Anisotropic propagation
  \eqid{RSCS-O.17} \EST{} $\to$ mode ladder \DER};
\node[box, below=of prop] (coup) {Coupling / avoided crossing
  \eqid{RSCS-O.4} \EST--\DER};
\node[box, below=of coup] (node) {Node menu (8 definitions; measured
  node supersedes) \EST/\HYP};
\node[box, below=of node] (probe) {Probes: acoustic, electrical, optical
  \DER{} predictions};
\node[box, below=of probe] (claims) {Pre-registered claims
  H-20\,--\,H-28 \HYP/\ENG{} with failure conditions};
\foreach \a/\b in {geo/mat, mat/prop, prop/coup, coup/node, node/probe,
                   probe/claims}{\draw[arr] (\a) -- (\b);}
\end{tikzpicture}
\caption{Forward hierarchy: measurement feeds models; models feed
predictions; predictions feed pre-registered falsification rows. Nothing
flows backward from source lore into the model without a classification
boundary.}
\label{fig:hier}
\end{figure}

\section{Anisotropic propagation}
v2 used one scalar effective speed $\vL = \gvVtwoMean\,\pm 5\%$
\si{\meter\per\second} \eqid{RGCS-M.10}, explicitly a \HYP{} that a
single scalar suffices. v3 resolves it: the Christoffel eigenproblem
\begin{equation}
\Gamma_{ik} = c_{ijkl}\, n_j n_l, \qquad \Gamma\,\mathbf{u} = \rho
v^2\,\mathbf{u}, \qquad \eqid{RSCS-O.17}\ \EST
\end{equation}
with the handbook $\alpha$-quartz constants
\citep{bechmann1958,auld1973} ($\rho = \gvDensity$
\si{\kilogram\per\cubic\meter}, D5-002) yields the quasi-longitudinal
and quasi-shear speeds along any direction:
\input{tables/axis_speeds}
Along Z, $v_{qL} = \gvZql$~\si{\meter\per\second} --- \gvZqlDevPct\% from
the v2 default, inside its band $[\gvVtwoLo, \gvVtwoHi]$
\si{\meter\per\second} --- and along X, $v_{qL} =
\gvXql$~\si{\meter\per\second}. The v2 $\pm5\%$ band is thereby
\emph{explained} as the physical X--Z anisotropy spread rather than an
undeclared systematic (Figure~\ref{fig:sweep}). Conservative extension is
machine-tested.

\begin{figure}[H]
\centering
\includegraphics[width=0.74\linewidth]{anisotropy_sweep}
\caption{$v_{qL}$ versus propagation direction from X toward Z
\eqid{RSCS-O.17}. The shaded region is the frozen v2 scalar band; the
anisotropic curve stays within it, recovering the scalar model as the
orientation-unknown special case (model-selection rule
Section~\ref{sec:model}).}
\label{fig:sweep}
\end{figure}

\section{The node menu}\label{sec:node}
``The eye'' of the historical source material is treated as a
\emph{menu of eight competing definitions}, each classified, none
presumed: (1) metric centre \EST, (2) prismatic-shaft prior \DER, (3)
measured vibration node \EST{} (measured; supersedes all others, v2
H-07), (4) electrical node \HYP{} (H-24), (5) optical path/phase feature
\HYP{} (H-25), (6) maximal multimode overlap \HYP{} (H-26), (7)
coupling-integral maximum \HYP{} (H-27), (8) phase singularity/saddle
\HYP{} (H-28). Rows H-24\,--\,H-28 each carry an observable, controls,
and a pre-registered failure condition; a definition that fails is
retired, not reinterpreted.

\section{Optical probe layer}
Quartz is uniaxial positive: $n_o = \gvNo$, $n_e = \gvNe$
($\Delta n = \gvBirefringence$ at \SI{589.3}{\nano\meter})
\citep{hecht2017}. Three conventional mechanisms give \DER{}
predictions:
\begin{enumerate}
\item \textbf{Photoelastic phase modulation}: $\Delta n = -\tfrac12 n^3
p S$ \citep{narasimhamurty1981}; with $p_{11}$ and a typical acoustic
strain $S = 10^{-7}$, $\Delta n = \gvPelDn$ --- small but standard
interferometric territory.
\item \textbf{Birefringence modulation} between crossed polarizers.
\item \textbf{Photothermal absorption} --- an artifact channel, bounded
by a matched-power off-node control (H-22 \ENG).
\end{enumerate}
The acousto-optic figure of merit $M_2 = n^6 p^2/(\rho v^3) = \gvMTwo$
\si{\second\cubed\per\kilogram} (computed with the anisotropic X-axis
speed) is $10^2$--$10^3\times$ below dedicated acousto-optic materials:
an honest sensitivity bound. A straight-ray entry-facet model answers
the geometric question --- an optical path \emph{can} address any
interior target, with the geometric centre, predicted node, and measured
node kept as three distinct locations. Directionality: an unbiased
passive quartz path is reciprocal; H-21 and H-23 are pre-registered
\emph{null} expectations (D6-003), and any surviving asymmetry would
first face a conventional-mechanism audit
\citep{wang2025selfinduced,chao2025tmoke}.

\section{Model selection: earning complexity}\label{sec:model}
Each layer must beat a simpler baseline before it is used: the
anisotropic model applies only when orientation is \emph{measured}
(else the scalar band); the compact-coordinate spectrum only if it beats
a plain rod on held-out modes (v2 H-01); the coupled-mode model only in
the strong-coupling regime; the measured node supersedes every prior.
Complexity that does not change a prediction is refused.

\section{Environmental and fixture factors}
Surface finish, inclusions, electrodes, fixtures, hand loading (v2,
frozen), thermal drift of the elastic constants
($\sim 10^{-4}/\mathrm{K}$ \DER) and humidity/adsorbate mass loading are
declared inputs of the crystal database, each with an uncertainty; the
experiment programme's thermal control branch bounds them.

\section{Status}
Implemented and machine-tested: anisotropic propagation, quasi-mode
taxonomy, optical constants and estimates, ray/path model, crystal
database with schema migration, FEA material-card export. Pre-registered
and awaiting bench work: H-20\,--\,H-28. No physical hypothesis of the
programme is confirmed; this application ships the models, the
uncertainty discipline, and the falsification plan.
